action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 6 LightGBM underperformed the 3-epoch MLP baseline (ic_mean 0.0141 vs 0.0153), likely due to severe underfitting (only 17 trees) and no hyperparameter tuning. Analysis: MLP is the better candidate so far, with val_ic reaching 0.038 but test_ic only 0.015, suggesting room for improvement via capacity, regularization, and longer training. Decision: Scale up MLP significantly (hidden_dim=1024, dropout=0.3, weight_decay=1e-4), train for 30 epochs with early stopping, clip extreme labels, and save model architecture config so predict.py can load it correctly. Back up the weak LGB checkpoint to isolate MLP performance.
action.command: mv checkpoints/lgb_model.txt checkpoints/lgb_model.txt.bak 2>/dev/null || true; python train.py --epochs 30 --hidden_dim 1024 --dropout 0.3 --weight_decay 1e-4 --label_clip 0.1 --patience 5 && python predict.py --data_path local_test_eval --checkpoint checkpoints --output local_test_eval/output.json
